    <item>
      <title>Signs point to Steve Kerr leaving Golden State Warriors</title>
      <description>Golden State Warriors coach Steve Kerr says he won't decide on his coaching future for "a week or two." Since the Warriors' season ended in a play-in loss to the Phoenix Suns on Friday, all the signs point to Kerr leaving the team he's coached for the last dozen seasons.</description>
      <pubDate>20 Apr 2026 21:38:57 -0400</pubDate>
      <link>https://www.yardbarker.com/nba/articles/signs_point_to_steve_kerr_leaving_golden_state_warriors/s1_13132_43751646</link>

        <media:credit> Isaiah J. Downing-Imagn Images </media:credit>
        <media:title> Golden State Warriors head coach Steve Kerr looks on in the first quarter against the Denver Nuggets at Ball Arena. </media:title>
      </media:content>
      <category>NBA</category>
      <category>Golden State Warriors</category>
      <category>Steve Kerr</category>
      <category>Steph Curry</category>
      <category>Draymond Green</category>
    </item>

    <item>
      <title>Brandon Ingram disappears in Raptors' Game 2 loss to Cavaliers</title>
      <description>When they traded for Brandon Ingram and gave him a big contract extension a year ago, the Toronto Raptors thought he was the missing piece. Monday night against the Cleveland Cavaliers, he was just missing.</description>
      <pubDate>21 Apr 2026 01:14:07 -0400</pubDate>

        <media:credit> David Dermer-Imagn Images </media:credit>
        <media:title> Toronto Raptors forward Brandon Ingram (3) looks to pass during the second half of Game 2 of the first round of the 2026 NBA Playoffs against the Cleveland Cavaliers at Rocket Arena.  </media:title>
      </media:content>

    <item>
      <title>Hawks' CJ McCollum becomes newest MSG villain in Game 2 win over Knicks</title>

    <item>
      <title>All-Star duo leads Nuggets to ugly Game 1 win over Timberwolves</title>
      <description>The Denver Nuggets couldn't make threes, lost Aaron Gordon to foul trouble and fell behind by double digits early. Thanks to patience, defense and their All-Star duo, Denver took Game One over the Minnesota Timberwolves anyway.</description>
      <pubDate>21 Apr 2026 01:35:27 -0400</pubDate>
      <link>https://www.yardbarker.com/nba/articles/all_star_duo_leads_nuggets_to_ugly_game_1_win_over_timberwolves/s1_13132_43744454</link>
      <guid>https://www.yardbarker.com/nba/articles/all_star_duo_leads_nuggets_to_ugly_game_1_win_over_timberwolves/s1_13132_43744454</guid>
      <author>Sean Keane, Yardbarker</author>
      <media:content url="http://www.yardbarker.com/media/6/a/6a3b9fb18e21f0f89d641f5aa18111c4bdf2fd0b/thumb_16x9/star-duo-leads-nuggets-ugly-game-1-win-over.jpg">
        <media:credit> Christopher Hanewinckel-Imagn Images </media:credit>
        <media:title> Denver Nuggets center Nikola Jokic (15) is fouled by Minnesota Timberwolves guard Anthony Edwards (5) as he handles the ball during the first half in game one of the first round of the 2026 NBA Playoffs at Ball Arena.  </media:title>
      </media:content>
      <category>NBA</category>
      <category>Denver Nuggets</category>
      <category>Minnesota Timberwolves</category>
      <category>Nikola Jokic</category>
      <category>Aaron Gordon</category>
      <category>Jamal Murray</category>
    </item>

      <title>New Trail Blazers owner showing troubling signs of cheapness</title>

    <item>
      <title>Joel Embiid-less 76ers look like they stand little chance against Jayson Tatum-led Celtics in Game 1 loss</title>
      <description>Tatum has reminded the Boston Celtics why they missed him dearly during last season's NBA playoffs.</description>
      <pubDate>19 Apr 2026 22:54:35 -0400</pubDate>
      <link>https://www.yardbarker.com/nba/articles/joel_embiid_less_76ers_look_like_they_stand_little_chance_against_jayson_tatum_led_celtics_in_game_1_loss/s1_13132_43747315</link>
